questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I have been working here from 2015.
options: I have been working here since 2015.
I have been working here from the year 2015.
I have been working here from 2015.
questions.solution: I have been working here since 2015.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: corruption / development / hinders / economic
questions.solution: Corruption hinders economic development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
options: I would rather tea than coffee.
I would rather have tea than coffee.
I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
I prefer tea rather than coffee.
questions.solution: I would rather have tea than coffee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: performance / regular / improves / employee / training
questions.solution: Regular training improves employee performance

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is smarter than any student in the class.
options: She is the smartest than any student in the class.
She is smarter than any other student in the class.
She is smarter than any student in the class.
She is smarter than all student in the class.
questions.solution: She is smarter than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: safety / should / mines / our / be / in / priority / top
options: Safety in mines should be our top priority
In mines safety our top priority should be
Top priority safety should be in mines
Safety should be our top priority in mines
questions.solution: Safety should be our top priority in mines

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: One of the miners have been hospitalised.
options: One of the miners has been hospitalised.
One of the miners is being hospitalised.
One of the miners are hospitalised.
One of the miners were hospitalised.
questions.solution: One of the miners has been hospitalised.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: responsible / are / for / who / safety / miners / ensuring
options: Responsible for miners safety who is
Miners safety who is responsible for ensuring
Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety
Ensuring safety for miners who is responsible
questions.solution: Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She lost not only her wallet but also.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: quality / compromising / without / costs / reduce / should
options: Costs should reduce without compromising quality
Quality compromising without reduce costs should
Compromising quality without costs should reduce
Should reduce costs without compromising quality
questions.solution: Costs should reduce without compromising quality

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on me for being late.
options: She was angry at me for being late.
She was angry upon me for being late.
She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: company / its / announced / expansion / has / plans / the
options: The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans
Expansion plans the company has announced
Announced the company its expansion plans has
questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: It is high time we leave for the station.
options: It is high time we leave for the station.
It is high time we have left for the station.
It is high time we were leaving for the station.
It is high time we left for the station.
questions.solution: It is high time we left for the station.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: climate / urgent / change / requires / action / addressing
options: Addressing climate change requires urgent action
Climate change addressing urgent action requires
Requires urgent action change addressing climate
Urgent action climate change requires addressing
questions.solution: Addressing climate change requires urgent action

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
options: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of AMICABLE:
questions.solution: Hostile

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To see eye to eye':
questions.solution: To agree with someone

questions.stem: Correct: No sooner he arrived than the meeting started.
options: No sooner did he arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he arrives than the meeting started.
No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
questions.solution: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be under the weather':
questions.solution: To feel ill

questions.stem: Correct: He has been working here since five years.
options: He has been working here for five years.
He has worked here since five years.
He worked here since five years.
He is working here since five years.
questions.solution: He has been working here for five years.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To make no bones about':
questions.solution: To speak frankly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The team is working on the project since May.
options: The team had been working on the project since May.
The team was working on the project since May.
The team has worked on the project since May.
The team has been working on the project since May.
questions.solution: The team has been working on the project since May.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of MAGNANIMOUS:
questions.solution: Stingy

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Make-to-order (MTO):
options: Produce after customer order. High customization, long lead time. No FG inventory
questions.solution: MTO: engineer-to-order (ETO) for complex. Assemble-to-order (ATO): modules pre-built.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Make-or-buy decision: outsourcing favored when:
options: Core competency is not in that activity
questions.solution: Make-or-buy: outsource non-core, in-house core. Consider: strategic importance, quality, cost, capacity, IP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 2000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹4/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.9) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.85) and C (0.95). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Work sampling error depends on:
options: Number of observations and desired confidence level
questions.solution: Error = z * sqrt(p(1-p)/n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 30000/(100 − 60) = 750 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Breakeven point in a multi-product firm depends on:
questions.solution: Multi-product BEP: FC / weighted average CM. Sales mix is critical assumption. Changes in mix change BEP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD):
options: Average of |actual - forecast| over n periods
questions.solution: MAD: Σ|e_t|/n. Simple measure of forecast accuracy. No cancellation of errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse slotting optimization:
options: Class-based turnover: fast movers (golden zone, waist level). Pareto: 20% SKU = 80% orders
questions.solution: Slotting: cube movement (pick frequency × cube). Forward pick area replenishment. Batch size.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NIOSH lifting equation: RWL = LC × HM × VM × DM × AM × FM × CM. LC (load constant) =
questions.solution: NIOSH: recommended weight limit. LC = 23 kg (maximum under ideal conditions). Other factors reduce this.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Takt time formula:
options: Available production time / Customer demand rate
questions.solution: Takt: heartbeat of production. Matches pace to customer demand. Available time / demand = cycle time target.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Robust Design: signal-to-noise ratio measures:
questions.solution: S/N ratio: larger is better (for nominal-is-best). Maximizes mean while minimizing variance. Taguchi method.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parallel reliability: R₁=0.9, R₂=0.9. R_parallel=

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Frozen period in MPS:
options: Cannot be changed without management approval
questions.solution: Frozen: lock period for stability. Master scheduler cannot change without VP approval. Provides stability for execution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 1000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.8 and 0.7 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cognitive load theory three types of load:
questions.solution: Intrinsic: task complexity. Extraneous: presentation. Germane: learning.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Work sampling uses:
options: Random observations to estimate activity proportions
questions.solution: Work sampling: random instantaneous observations.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simulation optimization: OptQuest uses:
options: Metaheuristics (tabu search, neural networks, scatter search)
questions.solution: OptQuest: commercial optimization tool. Combines tabu, neural nets, scatter search. Finds near-optimal settings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dispatching:
options: Releasing orders to production. Job card, move card, inspection card
questions.solution: Dispatching: authorization to start. Centralized vs decentralized.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Maintainability: mean time to repair (MTTR) is related to:
questions.solution: MTTR: average repair time. Maintainability = P(T ≤ t) = 1 - e^(-t/MTTR). Design for accessibility.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard time is obtained from normal time by adding:
questions.solution: Standard time = normal time × (1 + allowance fraction).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Network simplex method: specialized for:
questions.solution: Network simplex: exploits network (graph) structure. Much faster than general simplex for network LP problems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 3+7, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing theory: M/M/1 steady-state probability P_n = (1-ρ)ρ^n where ρ = λ/μ. For stability:
questions.solution: ρ = λ/μ < 1 for stability. If ρ ≥ 1, queue grows infinitely. Average queue length L = ρ/(1-ρ).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Supply chain visibility provides:
options: Real-time tracking of materials and products
questions.solution: Visibility: end-to-end tracking enables proactive management.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 48 min, 6 stations and a cycle time of 10 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 48/(6×10) = 80%.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Line of balance used for:
questions.solution: LOB: graphical scheduling for repetitive production.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹10000, price = ₹100/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Leagile supply chain:
options: Leagile: lean upstream (efficient), agile downstream (responsive). Decoupling point
questions.solution: Leagile: combined lean + agile. Forecast-driven upstream, demand-driven downstream.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the performance rating of a worker is above 100%, the normal time will be:
questions.solution: Normal = observed × rating; a rating >100% (faster than standard pace) raises normal time above observed.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Linear regression forecasting assumes:
options: Linear relationship between time and variable
questions.solution: Y = a + bt. Least squares fit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC phases:
options: Define problem, Measure baseline, Analyze root cause, Improve process, Control gains
questions.solution: Define: project charter, SIPOC, VOC. Measure: data collection plan. Analyze: fishbone, FMEA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Decision theory:
options: States of nature, alternatives, payoff matrix. Maximax, maximin, minimax regret, expected value
questions.solution: Maximax (optimistic), Maximin (pessimistic), Minimax regret (Savage), Expected value (risk-neutral).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simplex method: pivot column selected by most negative reduced cost. Pivot row by:
options: Minimum ratio test (RHS / pivot column element, positive only)
questions.solution: Ratio test: min(b_i/a_ik) where a_ik>0. Determines leaving variable. Prevents infeasibility.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation problem:
options: Minimize cost of shipping from sources to destinations. NWC → MODI
questions.solution: Transportation: balanced (supply = demand) or unbalanced. NW corner, least cost, VAM for initial.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Integer programming:
options: Decision variables integer (binary). Branch and bound, cutting planes. MIP
questions.solution: IP: facility location, scheduling, assignment. B&B: LP relaxation, if not integer → branch. Cutting planes: Gomory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic programming: Bellman's principle of optimality:
options: Optimal policy has the property that whatever the initial state and decision, remaining decisions must constitute optimal policy for remaining problem
questions.solution: Optimal substructure: optimal solution contains optimal sub-solutions. Overlapping subproblems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Attribute gage study:
options: Assess agreement: Kappa statistic. κ > 0.9 = excellent. Validates go/no-go gages
questions.solution: Cohen's Kappa: chance-corrected agreement. κ = (p_o - p_e)/(1 - p_e). Kappa > 0.75 excellent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹4000 and crash time/cost are 5 days/₹8000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (8000−4000)/(10−5) = ₹800/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ROP: demand=200/day, lead time=2 days, safety stock=50. ROP=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹40/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Resource-constrained project scheduling (RCPSP):
options: NP-hard. Heuristics: serial (priority rule) vs parallel schedule generation. LFT, MINSLACK, GRPW
questions.solution: RCPSP: limited resource capacity. Serial SGS: schedule one activity at a time. Heuristic + metaheuristic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP):
options: Monthly review. Demand plan, supply plan, financials. Executive meeting
questions.solution: S&OP: cross-functional. Four phases: data, demand, supply, pre-meeting, executive S&OP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — degeneracy:
options: Basic variable = 0. Tie in ratio. May cycle. Bland's rule avoids cycling
questions.solution: Degeneracy: same objective value at different bases. Starred degeneracy. Anti-cycling rules.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process chart — outline process chart:
options: Shows operations + inspections. No transports, delays, storage
questions.solution: Outline: overall flow. Flow process chart: includes all 5 symbols (O, ⇒, □, D, ∇).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹10000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In acceptance sampling, which pairing is correct?
options: Type I error → accepting a bad lot
Type I error → rejecting a good lot (producer's risk)
Type II error → rejecting a good lot
questions.solution: Producer's risk (α) rejects good lots; consumer's risk (β) accepts bad lots.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Predictive maintenance — oil analysis:
options: Wear particle, contamination, oil degradation. Spectroscopy, ferrography
questions.solution: Oil: Fe, Cu, Al, Pb (wear). Si (dirt). TAN/TBN (degradation). Particle count (ISO 4406).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse layout: forward pick area vs reserve storage:
options: Forward: small, fast picks. Reserve: bulk, replenish forward
questions.solution: Forward pick: golden zone, fast movers. Reserve: bulk storage, replenishment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In single exponential smoothing, the new forecast is:
questions.solution: Ft+1 = α·At + (1−α)·Ft; it is a weighted average favouring recent data as α rises.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Push vs pull systems:
questions.solution: Push: make-to-stock. Pull: make-to-order. Hybrid: push-pull boundary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period — underage cost Cu:
options: P(shortage) = Cu/(Cu+Co). Profit lost per unit under-ordered
questions.solution: Newsvendor: Cu = marginal profit (p-c). Co = cost - salvage (c-s). Service level = Cu/(Cu+Co).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 8 min with 20% allowances. Its standard time is:
questions.solution: Standard time = normal × (1 + allowance) = 8×(1 + 20/100) = 9.6 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multi-echelon inventory:
options: Central warehouse + regional warehouses + retail. Bullwhip effect management
questions.solution: Multi-echelon: coordination. Base stock policy. Risk pooling. Postponement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 4 min at a performance rating of 100%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 4×100% = 4 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 800 units using 50 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 800/50 = 16 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 60, lead time = 5 days, safety stock = 80 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 60×5 + 80 = 380 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 600 units using 80 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 600/80 = 7.5 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 5000 units, ordering cost = ₹40/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 4 min with 15% allowances. Its standard time is:
questions.solution: Standard time = normal × (1 + allowance) = 4×(1 + 15/100) = 4.6 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 20 units/day with a lead time of 3 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 20×3 = 60 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root cause analysis (RCA) — 5 Whys:
options: Iterative questioning. Causal chain: physical root → human root → latent root. Corrective action for each
questions.solution: 5 Whys: Toyota Production System. Latent root: organizational factors. Fix root → prevent recurrence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Exponential smoothing: F_t = αD_{t-1} + (1-α)F_{t-1}. For α=0.2, D=100, F=95:
questions.solution: EMA: weighted average. α=0.2: 20% weight to latest, 80% to history. More smoothing with low α.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 2000/yr, order cost ₹40 and order quantity 200, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (2000/200)×40 = ₹400.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PMTS — MTM-1:
options: Reach, Move, Grasp, Position, Release, Disengage, etc. TMU per motion
questions.solution: MTM-1: basic motions. TMU = 0.00001 hour = 0.036 sec. MTM-2, MTM-3: simpler.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability — exponential distribution:
options: Memoryless. Constant failure rate. MTBF = 1/λ. R(t) = e^(-λt). Good for random failures
questions.solution: Exponential: simple. Often used for electronic components. For mechanical: Weibull more accurate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 1000 units, ordering cost = ₹20/order, holding cost = ₹4/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing theory — M/M/1:
options: Poisson arrival (λ), exponential service (μ). L = λ/(μ-λ). W = 1/(μ-λ)
questions.solution: M/M/1: L_q = λ²/(μ(μ-λ)). ρ = λ/μ < 1 for stability. Little's law: L = λW.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hypothesis testing — p-value:
options: Probability of observing data if H₀ is true. p < 0.05 → reject H₀ at 95% confidence
questions.solution: Type I: reject true H₀ (α). Type II: fail to reject false H₀ (β). Power = 1-β.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Holt's method:
options: Exponential smoothing with trend (level + trend components)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 4000/yr, order cost ₹50 and order quantity 400, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (4000/400)×50 = ₹500.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 100, 130 and 140 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ISO 9001:2015 key requirements:
options: Context of the organization, leadership, planning, support, operation, performance evaluation, improvement
questions.solution: ISO 9001:2015: risk-based thinking, process approach, PDCA cycle. Certification by external body.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Natural tolerance limits:
questions.solution: Cp > 1.33 = capable. Cp > 1.67 = excellent. Cp < 1 = incapable. Cpk = min[(USL-μ)/3σ, (μ-LSL)/3σ].

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gage R&R — %GRR acceptance:
options: <10%: excellent. 10-30%: marginal (depends on risk). >30%: unacceptable. ndc > 4
questions.solution: %GRR = σ_GRR/σ_total. ndc = number of distinct categories = √2 × σ_part/σ_GRR. ndc ≥ 5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — p chart:
options: Proportion defective. Attribute data (pass/fail). Variable sample size
questions.solution: p-chart: UCL = p̄ + 3√(p̄(1-p̄)/n). LCL = p̄ - 3√(p̄(1-p̄)/n). For defectives.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT — variance along critical path:
options: σ²_cp = Σσ²_i (sum of critical activity variances). Std dev = √(σ²_cp)
questions.solution: Central limit theorem on CP. Probability: Z = (T_s - T_e)/σ_cp. Normal distribution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Markov — steady state:
options: π = π·P. Eigenvector of transpose of P with eigenvalue 1. Ergodic: unique steady state
questions.solution: Steady state: independent of initial state (if ergodic). π_i > 0, sum π_i = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Branch and bound: lower bound for maximization problem comes from:
options: LP relaxation of the current node (best feasible solution found)
questions.solution: Max: LP relaxation gives upper bound. Feasible IP solution gives lower bound. Prune if UB < LB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 50 units/day with a lead time of 3 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 50×3 = 150 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kanban system:
options: Card signal for production/pull. Number of kanbans = (D·L·(1+α))/C
questions.solution: Kanban: withdrawal and production kanban. α = safety factor. C = container capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4, tm = 5, tp = 14 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regression forecasting: y = a + bx. Slope b =
questions.solution: Least squares: b = Σ(x-x̄)(y-ȳ)/Σ(x-x̄)². a = ȳ - bx̄. R² = explained variance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast combination — simple average:
options: Combining ≥2 forecasts often beats individual. Average, weighted (by inverse MSE), or trimmed
questions.solution: Combination: reduces variance. Bates-Granger (1969). Weighted: w_i = (1/MSE_i)/Σ(1/MSE_j).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die): goal is to:
options: Reduce setup/changeover time to under 10 minutes
questions.solution: SMED: Shingo. Separate internal (machine stop) from external (running) setup. Convert internal to external. Streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 6 min at a performance rating of 80%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 6×80% = 4.8 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 8, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) path planning:
options: Unidirectional or bidirectional paths. Zone control or continuous path
questions.solution: AGV: wire, magnetic tape, or free-ranging (laser, vision). Traffic management required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Just-in-Time (JIT) vs Just-in-Case (JIC):
options: JIT: minimize inventory, respond to demand. JIC: buffer inventory for uncertainty
questions.solution: JIT: lean, low waste. JIC: risk-averse, high inventory. Trade-off: cost vs service.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 48 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 48/(5×12) = 80%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹80, variable cost ₹40/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 30000/(80 − 40) = 750 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecasting — drift method:
options: Naive + average historical change. F_{t+h} = A_t + h·(A_t-A_1)/(t-1)
questions.solution: Drift: captures trend. Average change per period. Between naive and linear regression.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Measurement System Analysis: Gage R&R studies:
options: Variability from measurement system vs total variability
questions.solution: Gage R&R: %GRR < 10% acceptable, 10-30% marginal, >30% unacceptable. Studies repeatability and reproducibility.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT differs from CPM mainly in that PERT treats activity times as:
questions.solution: PERT uses optimistic, most-likely and pessimistic estimates; CPM uses single deterministic times.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Follow-up (expediting):
options: Tracking progress, identifying delays, corrective action
questions.solution: Follow-up: ensure delivery on time. Gantt chart progress bars. Hot list for priorities.
